title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University's Joseph Aoun earned $3,045,428 in FY 2025, making him the highest-paid college president in Massachusetts.
Aoun's compensation rose significantly due to $700,000 in deferred compensation and $550,000 in bonuses and incentives.
MIT's Sally Kornbluth follows as second highest-paid, earning $2,151,788, while seven other presidents earned over $1 million.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street in Cambridge.
Emergency responders declared Bautista dead at the scene after a pedestrian reported a body at 5:30 a.m.
Cambridge police and state detectives are investigating the shooting; anonymous tips can be submitted via phone or email.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from the New England Aquarium assisted in hand-rearing penguin chicks in South Africa with SANCCOB.
African penguins are critically endangered, facing extinction by 2035 due to ocean changes, fisheries, and habitat loss.
The New England Aquarium joined AZA's SAFE African Penguin program in 2023, supporting conservation and public education efforts.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras appeals a seven-game suspension for involvement in a fight with Nationals' pitcher Cade Cavalli.
Contreras faces emotional distress due to Venezuela earthquakes, affecting his on-field conduct and performances.
Contreras apologizes for recent confrontations, expressing a desire to demonstrate better judgment and character.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez from the Boston Red Sox selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star game.
Chapman has 17 saves with a 2.10 ERA and 33 strikeouts over 25.2 innings this season.
Suarez has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings, returning to Philadelphia for the All-Star game.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers, feeling disrespected by the decision.
Paul Pierce expressed disappointment about the trade but encouraged Brown to use the disrespect as motivation for future success.
Pierce, once traded from the Celtics himself, believes Boston can still succeed with its current team including newcomer Paul George.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston hosted a Fourth of July parade and a Declaration of Independence reading to celebrate the 250th anniversary.
The Independence Day Parade featured participants like the Wilmington Minutemen and the William Diamond Junior Fife & Drum Corps.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular included performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper, and began with a drone show.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in home runs, OPS, and ranks second in RBIs and hits over the last 30 days.
Durbin's performance has improved since May, raising his batting average from .163 to .321 by adjusting his training approach.
Durbin is signed on a one-year deal but under team control until 2031, providing roster flexibility for the Red Sox.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set an MLB record with his 1,364th strikeout as a relief pitcher, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm.
Chapman achieved the milestone during a 5-2 victory over the Los Angeles Angels, striking out Denzer Guzman.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's achievement with a video montage in the clubhouse after the game.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked as the most collision-prone city in the U.S., with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, rank among the bottom five with over 5 years between collisions, according to Allstate.
Providence, RI, is listed among the top 10 riskiest cities, with 5.87 years between collisions.
